title: All about aRboretum
text: aRboretum integrated R package designed automate production botanical collection labels, taxonomic data extraction interactive web labels print-ready QR codes. can used arboreta, botanic gardens, educational trails, living plant collections. package offers: multilingual output Portuguese, English, French, Spanish; synonym resolution integration data Flora e Funga Brasil (FFB) Plants World Online (POWO); interactive HTML labels species information, text--speech, distribution maps, photo slideshows, optional personal audio recordings; searchable minisite index linking generated labels; printable QR-code labels different layouts; one flexible additional language slot community-based accessibility add_lang. functions designed work together. vignette provides technical overview package workflow, main arguments, expected data structure, common customisation options.

title: aRboretum
text: package provides three main functions: arboretum_data() - retrieves merges species data Flora e Funga Brasil Plants World Online. arboretum_audios() - creates folders HTML recording guide custom personal audio files, including one optional extra community local language. arboretum_photos() — creates folders personal species photos. arboretum_labels() - generates interactive multilingual, audio-enhanced HTML species labels, support one optional extra language supplied directly input data. arboretum_minisite - creates searchable multilingual index page generated species labels. arboretum_qrcodes() — generates printable QR-code labels linked species pages source URLs.

title: aRboretum
text: ’s complete example data extraction custom audio labels:
code: # Step 1: Extract species data my_species <- c(   "Euterpe edulis",   "Paubrasilia echinata",   "Coffea arabica",   "Luetzelburgia bahiensis" )  species_data <- arboretum_data(   spp_list = my_species,   verbose = TRUE,   save = TRUE,   format = "xlsx",   filename = "my_garden_plants",   dir = "data" )  # Step 2: Create folder structure for personal audio arboretum_audios(   data_path = "data/my_garden_plants.xlsx",   printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),   verbose = TRUE )  # Step 3: Add your own recordings! # Place files like: #   arboretum_personal_audios/ARECACEAE_Euterpe_edulis_EN/my_recording.mp3 #   arboretum_personal_audios/ARECACEAE_Euterpe_edulis_PT/gravacao_pessoal.wav  # Step 4: Prepare personal photo folders  arboretum_photos(   data_path = "data/my_garden_plants.xlsx",    verbose = TRUE )  # Step 5: Generate HTML labels (automatically uses your recordings) arboretum_labels(   data_path = "data/my_garden_plants.xlsx",   printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),   rate = 0.9,   path_to_logo = "my_garden_logo.png",   dir = "labels" )  # Step 6: Create a multilingual minisite index arboretum_minisite(   labels_dir = "labels",   site_title = "My Botanical Garden Collection",   printed_lang = c("pt", "en"),   group_by_family = TRUE,   logo = "my_garden_logo.png",   verbose = TRUE )  # Step 7: Generate QR-code labels linked to the published minisite  arboretum_qrcodes(   data_path = "data/my_garden_plants.xlsx",    base_url = "https://myinstitution.org/my-collection",    layout = "complete",    printed_lang = "pt",    path_to_logo = "my_garden_logo.png",    format = "pdf",    verbose = TRUE )  # Step 8: Deploy to an internet pages # Copy the entire 'minisite' folder to your internet page repository # The index.html file will automatically show all species!  # Labels are now available in the 'labels' directory # Open in any modern browser to see distribution maps and hear your recordings!

title: Extract and compile flora data from multiple taxonomic databases — arboretum_data
text: dataframe combining data retrieved FFB POWO. returned columns include: family: Family name accepted taxon. genus: Genus extracted accepted scientific name. taxonName: Accepted scientific name synonym resolution. scientificNameAuthorship: Authorship string. FFB.vernacularName: Vernacular names FFB, multiple values concatenated " | ". country: Country names FFB derived POWO botanical countries. endemism: Endemism status Brazil FFB, inferred POWO country-level distribution FFB data unavailable. botanical_country: Native distribution based POWO botanical countries. introduced_to: Countries botanical regions species introduced according POWO. FFB.establishmentMeans: Establishment means FFB, "Native", "Cultivated", "Naturalized". FFB.stateProvince: Brazilian states FFB, concatenated " | ". FFB.phytogeographicDomain: Brazilian phytogeographic domains FFB, translated English applicable concatenated " | ". FFB.vegetationType: Vegetation types FFB, concatenated " | ". IUCN.status: IUCN conservation status retrieved POWO, available. FFB.genusRichness: Number accepted species genus recorded FFB. FFB.genusRank: Rank genus species richness FFB, 1 representing richest genus. plant_uses_EN, plant_uses_PT, plant_uses_ES, plant_uses_FR: Plant-use fields English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, reserved downstream annotation future use. free_notes_EN, free_notes_PT, free_notes_ES, free_notes_FR: Free-text note fields English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, reserved downstream annotation future use. POWO.url: URL species page POWO. FFB.url: URL species page FFB. species found one database, fields missing database returned NA. Species found either database omitted final dataframe. overlapping fields, FFB data generally take precedence, except selected POWO-derived fields botanical country, introduced range, IUCN status, POWO URL.

title: Extract and compile flora data from multiple taxonomic databases — arboretum_data
text: function follows four main steps: Flora e Funga Brasil data extraction Downloads latest FFB Darwin Core Archive using floraR::flora_download(). Parses archive floraR::flora_parse(). Extracts processes taxon, distribution, vernacular name, species profile tables. Matches queried species name FFB taxon table. Resolves synonyms accepted names possible. Retrieves family, accepted name, authorship, vernacular names, distribution, endemism, establishment means, Brazilian states, phytogeographic domains, vegetation types, FFB reference URLs. POWO data extraction Searches POWO using taxize::pow_search(). Resolves synonyms accepted names possible. Retrieves detailed information using taxize::pow_lookup(), including taxonomy, distribution, introduced range, IUCN status, POWO URL. Converts POWO botanical countries standard country names using internal helper function. Data merging Combines FFB POWO results single dataframe. Prioritizes FFB data overlapping fields available. Complements FFB records POWO botanical countries, introduced range, conservation status, POWO URLs. Infers endemism POWO country-level distribution FFB endemism data unavailable. Genus-level summaries Extracts genus accepted taxon name. Calculates number accepted species per genus FFB. Adds genus richness genus rank final dataframe. Adds multilingual genus curiosity notes using internal helper functions, available. processing, spp_list cleaned using internal helper functions. Leading trailing whitespace removed, names standardized, element checked ensure contains space. function stops error element appears binomial species name. save = TRUE, function creates output directory needed saves resulting dataframe either CSV file using utils::write.csv() Excel file using openxlsx::write.xlsx(). verbose = TRUE, message reports saved file path. temporary FFB download folder named "flora_download" removed function exits.

POWO FFB data dynamic external resources, results may change across database versions query dates.
